Drug Coverage Limitations
Medicaid only provides reimbursement for drugs included on the New York State List of Medicaid Reimbursable Drugs
(unless provided by a facility which includes the cost of drugs in their all-inclusive rate). The following are examples of
drugs/drug uses which are not reimbursable by Medicaid in accordance with Policy and/or state or federal Legislation:
• Drugs used for the treatment of anorexia, weight loss or weight gain pursuant to SSA §1927(d)(2).
• Drugs for the treatment of sexual dysfunction pursuant to SSA §1927(d)(2), and Social Services Law §365-a(4)(f).
• Drugs without a federal rebate agreement pursuant to SSA §1927(a).
• Drugs indicated for cosmetic use or hair growth pursuant to SSA §1927(d)(2).
• Drugs that are not approved as safe and effective by the FDA pursuant to SSA §1927(d)(4)(C).

answers.headline_summary: The provider manual, with an effective date of October 1, 2025, is more recent than the newest policy update from February 1, 2024, and provides more detailed information on the general reimbursement process for drugs, including how pharmacies are reimbursed for medications. Policy documents specifically address naloxone coverage, including over-the-counter (OTC) naloxone, while the provider manual outlines general drug reimbursement principles.
answers.policy_definition: New York State (NYS) Medicaid covers naloxone over the counter (OTC) when prescribed by a practitioner for a NYS Medicaid member or dispensed to a NYS Medicaid member in compliance with the State-issued naloxone standing order [New York State Medicaid Update February 2024 Volume 40 Number 2:4, 5 — February 1, 2024]. Medicaid policy also requires Medicaid fee-for-service (FFS) and Medicaid Managed Care (MMC) to provide at least five days’ coverage for emergencies, without prior authorization, for medications used to treat substance use disorders, which would include naloxone [New York State Medicaid Update May 2020 Volume 36 Number 10:7, 8 — May 1, 2020].
answers.provider_manual_definition: The NYRx provides reimbursement for prescription drugs included on the NY Medicaid Pharmacy List of Reimbursable Drugs [New York State Medicaid Program: Pharmacy Manual - Policy Guidelines:31 — October 1, 2025]. Pharmacies are reimbursed for the medication when billed via National Drug Code (NDC) using the established reimbursement methodology [New York State Medicaid Program: Pharmacy Manual - Policy Guidelines:15 — October 1, 2025]. Reimbursement is only available for multiple source drugs contained on the List of Medicaid Reimbursable Drugs [New York State Medicaid Program: Pharmacy Manual - Policy Guidelines:33 — October 1, 2025]. Medicaid only provides reimbursement for drugs included on the New York State List of Medicaid Reimbursable Drugs, unless provided by a facility that includes the cost of drugs in their all-inclusive rate [New York State Medicaid Program: Pharmacy Manual - Policy Guidelines:28 — October 1, 2025].
similarities: Both sources indicate that reimbursement is tied to a list of approved drugs. The policy documents imply coverage for naloxone [New York State Medicaid Update February 2024 Volume 40 Number 2:4, 5 — February 1, 2024], while the provider manual explicitly states reimbursement for drugs on the NY Medicaid Pharmacy List of Reimbursable Drugs [New York State Medicaid Program: Pharmacy Manual - Policy Guidelines:31 — October 1, 2025].
differences: Policy documents specifically address naloxone, including its coverage when prescribed OTC or dispensed under a standing order [New York State Medicaid Update February 2024 Volume 40 Number 2:4, 5 — February 1, 2024], and mention emergency coverage for substance use disorder medications without prior authorization [New York State Medicaid Update May 2020 Volume 36 Number 10:7, 8 — May 1, 2020]. The provider manual, effective October 1, 2025, provides general guidelines for drug reimbursement, focusing on billing via NDC and the requirement for drugs to be on the List of Medicaid Reimbursable Drugs, without specific mention of naloxone or OTC coverage [New York State Medicaid Program: Pharmacy Manual - Policy Guidelines:15, 31, 33 — October 1, 2025].

policy.text:
May 2020 New York State Medicaid Update pg. 7

Reminder: Substance Use Disorder Drug Treatment:
Medicaid Managed Care and Fee-for-Service Coverage
Important Legislation Regarding Drugs to Treat Substance Use Disorders:
• Medicaid policy requires Medicaid fee-for-service (FFS) and Medicaid Managed Care (MMC) to provide
at least five days’ coverage for emergencies, without prior authorization, for medications used to
treat substance use disorders. This includes medication associated with the management of opioid
withdrawal and/or stabilization as well as medication used for opioid overdose reversal.
• As published in the August 2016 issue of the Medicaid Update, New York State Social Services Law
§364-j and Public Health Law §273, prohibit prior authorization under Medicaid FFS and MMC for
initial or renewal prescriptions for preferred or formulary forms of buprenorphine or injectable
naltrexone when used for detoxification or maintenance treatment of opioid addiction. The
frequency, quantity, and/or duration limits supported by the Food and Drug Administration (FDA) and
Compendia may continue to be applied.

answers.headline_summary: The provider manual, with an effective date of October 1, 2025, is more recent than the newest policy update from February 1, 2024, and provides more detailed information on the general reimbursement process for drugs. Both sources indicate that naloxone is covered by NYS Medicaid, including over-the-counter versions, but the provider manual offers specific billing instructions for drugs and their administration.
answers.policy_definition: New York State (NYS) Medicaid covers naloxone over the counter (OTC) when prescribed by a practitioner of a NYS Medicaid member or dispensed to a NYS Medicaid member in compliance with the State-issued naloxone standing order [New York State Medicaid Update February 2024 Volume 40 Number 2:4, 5 — February 01, 2024]. This coverage for OTC naloxone was expanded as of September 2023 [New York State Medicaid Update September 2023 Volume 39 Number 14:7, 8 — September 01, 2023]. Medicaid policy also requires Medicaid fee-for-service (FFS) and Medicaid Managed Care (MMC) to provide at least five days’ coverage for emergencies, without prior authorization, for medications used to treat substance use disorders, which includes naloxone [New York State Medicaid Update May 2020 Volume 36 Number 10:7, 8 — May 01, 2020].
answers.provider_manual_definition: Reimbursement for drugs, including naloxone, is available for those included on the NY Medicaid Pharmacy List of Reimbursable Drugs [New York State Medicaid Program: Pharmacy Manual - Policy Guidelines:31 — October 01, 2025]. Prescriptions must be dispensed and billed by a Medicaid enrolled pharmacy using the member’s individual Medicaid Client Identification Number (CIN) [New York State Medicaid Program: Pharmacy Manual - Policy Guidelines:31 — October 01, 2025]. For the drug itself, the claim must be submitted using the ordering prescriber’s NPI in the Prescriber ID field, and the pharmacy will be reimbursed for the medication when billed via National Drug Code (NDC) using the reimbursement methodology [New York State Medicaid Program: Pharmacy Manual - Policy Guidelines:15 — October 01, 2025]. If billing for both the drug and its administration, these claims must be submitted separately; for administration, the claim must use procedure code 96372 and the pharmacy’s NPI in the Prescriber field [New York State Medicaid Program: Pharmacy Manual - Policy Guidelines:15 — October 01, 2025].
similarities: Both sources indicate that naloxone is covered by NYS Medicaid [New York State Medicaid Update February 2024 Volume 40 Number 2:4, 5 — February 01, 2024], [New York State Medicaid Program: Pharmacy Manual - Policy Guidelines:31 — October 01, 2025].

Both sources imply that naloxone is a reimbursable drug under Medicaid, with the policy documents specifically mentioning OTC coverage and the provider manual detailing general drug reimbursement processes [New York State Medicaid Update February 2024 Volume 40 Number 2:4, 5 — February 01, 2024], [New York State Medicaid Program: Pharmacy Manual - Policy Guidelines:31 — October 01, 2025].
differences: The policy documents explicitly state that NYS Medicaid covers over-the-counter (OTC) naloxone when prescribed or dispensed under a standing order [New York State Medicaid Update February 2024 Volume 40 Number 2:4, 5 — February 01, 2024], whereas the provider manual focuses on general drug reimbursement without specifically mentioning OTC naloxone.

The provider manual provides detailed instructions on how to bill for the drug and its administration, including using the prescriber's NPI for the drug claim and a specific procedure code (96372) for administration claims [New York State Medicaid Program: Pharmacy Manual - Policy Guidelines:15 — October 01, 2025]. The policy documents do not include such specific billing instructions.

The policy documents mention a requirement for Medicaid FFS and MMC to provide at least five days' coverage for emergency medications for substance use disorders without prior authorization [New York State Medicaid Update May 2020 Volume 36 Number 10:7, 8 — May 01, 2020], a detail not present in the provided provider manual excerpts.

provider_manual.text:
Pharmacy
providers who receive a “no TPL reimbursement” must ensure that the claim was adjudicated within the TPL’s claim
process requirements. This includes submitting the claim under the appropriate coverage benefit, medical or
pharmacy, such as with the Healthcare Common Procedure Coding System (HCPCS) or by the National Drug Code
(NDC). Additionally, pharmacies not contracted with the member’s TPL Plan may need to attempt an override with the
plan, or enroll with the plan, or advise the member and prescriber of the need to change dispensing to a network
pharmacy.
It is the responsibility of the prescriber to prescribe, according to the plan’s formulary, and to pursue any claim issues
such as, but not limited to:
• Necessary prior authorizations or appeals, or
• Prescription alternatives (for a non-formulary or non-preferred drug) or
• Necessary changes (quantity or day supply, etc.) or
• Selecting an in-network pharmacy that is agreeable to the member, or
• Billing the appropriate benefit (medical vs. pharmacy or HCPCS vs NDC).
provider_manual.title: New York State Medicaid Program: Pharmacy Manual - Policy Guidelines
provider_manual.authority: New York State Department of Health

provider_manual.text:
All prescription drugs are reimbursed on a fee-for-service basis. Residential health care
facilities may:
• Operate an institutional pharmacy to provide these items or
• Contract with Medicaid enrolled pharmacies to provide these items to Medicaid members. The pharmacy
must be reimbursed by the facility for all non-prescription drugs and medical/surgical supplies. Prescription
drugs may be billed directly to Medicaid by the dispensing pharmacy on a fee-for-service basis.
Drugs billed directly to Medicaid are limited to prescription drugs included on the New York State Medicaid List of
Reimbursable Drugs: https://www.emedny.org/info/formfile.aspx and are subject to refill, frequency, quantity and
duration, step therapy, and prior authorization/approval requirements as described in this Manual.

Medicaid members with both Medicare and Medicaid (dual eligible Medicaid members) who have met their residency
requirements in a residential health care facility will receive their prescription drug coverage from their Medicare Part D
Plan.

answers.headline_summary: The provider manual, with an effective date of October 1, 2025, is more recent than the policy documents, which have a most recent effective date of February 1, 2024. Therefore, the provider manual should receive more weight regarding how naloxone is reimbursed. Both sources indicate that naloxone is covered by NYS Medicaid, with the provider manual detailing the specific billing procedures for the drug and its administration.
answers.policy_definition: New York State (NYS) Medicaid covers naloxone over the counter (OTC) when prescribed by a practitioner for a NYS Medicaid member or dispensed to a NYS Medicaid member in compliance with the State-issued naloxone standing order [New York State Medicaid Update February 2024 Volume 40 Number 2:4, 5 — 2024-02-01]. When dispensed OTC via a standing order, specific conditions apply: a NYS Medicaid member must have specifically requested the item or a pharmacist initiated dispensing based on a determination of need/risk, a pharmacy submits one course of therapy (two doses) with no refills, and the drug item(s) are dispensed according to FDA guidelines, NYS laws, rules, and regulations, and NYS Medicaid policy [New York State Medicaid Update February 2024 Volume 40 Number 2:5 — 2024-02-01]. Reimbursement is made in accordance with existing Medicaid policy related to supervision and billing rules and requirements [New York State Medicaid Update February 2019 Special Edition Volume 35 Number 2:6 — 2019-02-01].
answers.provider_manual_definition: Naloxone, as a prescription drug, is reimbursed on a fee-for-service basis [New York State Medicaid Program: Pharmacy Manual - Policy Guidelines:30, 31 — 2025-10-01]. For the drug itself, the claim must be submitted using the ordering prescriber’s NPI in the Prescriber ID field, and the pharmacy will be reimbursed for the medication when billed via National Drug Code (NDC) using reimbursement methodology [New York State Medicaid Program: Pharmacy Manual - Policy Guidelines:15 — 2025-10-01]. If billing for both the drug and its administration, these claims must be submitted separately [New York State Medicaid Program: Pharmacy Manual - Policy Guidelines:15 — 2025-10-01]. For the administration, the claim must be submitted using procedure code 96372, and the pharmacy’s NPI in the Prescriber ID field [New York State Medicaid Program: Pharmacy Manual - Policy Guidelines:15 — 2025-10-01]. Pharmacies must keep these orders on file [New York State Medicaid Program: Pharmacy Manual - Policy Guidelines:15 — 2025-10-01]. Reimbursement is only available for multiple source drugs on the List of Medicaid Reimbursable Drugs [New York State Medicaid Program: Pharmacy Manual - Policy Guidelines:33 — 2025-10-01].
similarities: Both sources confirm that naloxone is covered by NYS Medicaid [New York State Medicaid Update February 2024 Volume 40 Number 2:4, 5 — 2024-02-01], [New York State Medicaid Program: Pharmacy Manual - Policy Guidelines:15 — 2025-10-01].
differences: The provider manual specifies the billing procedures for naloxone, including separate claims for the drug (billed via NDC with ordering prescriber's NPI) and its administration (procedure code 96372 with pharmacy's NPI) [New York State Medicaid Program: Pharmacy Manual - Policy Guidelines:15 — 2025-10-01], whereas the policy documents generally state that reimbursement is made in accordance with existing Medicaid policy related to supervision and billing rules without detailing specific codes or NPI usage [New York State Medicaid Update February 2019 Special Edition Volume 35 Number 2:6 — 2019-02-01].

Policy documents explicitly mention coverage for over-the-counter (OTC) naloxone when prescribed or dispensed via a standing order, including specific conditions for dispensing such as member request or pharmacist-initiated dispensing, and limits on refills (one course of therapy, two doses) [New York State Medicaid Update February 2024 Volume 40 Number 2:4, 5 — 2024-02-01]. The provider manual does not specifically detail OTC naloxone coverage or these dispensing conditions, focusing more on general prescription drug reimbursement and billing mechanics [New York State Medicaid Program: Pharmacy Manual - Policy Guidelines:15, 30, 31 — 2025-10-01].

Reminder: Substance Use Disorder Drug Treatment,
Medicaid Managed Care and Fee-for-Service Coverage
Social Services Law §364-j and Public Health Law §273 prohibits prior authorization under Medicaid fee-for-
service (FFS) and Medicaid Managed Care (MMC) for initial or renewal prescriptions for preferred or formulary

addiction. The Food and Drug Administration (FDA) and Compendia-supported frequency quantity and/or
duration limits may continue to be applied.
The New York State (NYS) MMC Pharmacy Benefit Information Center
The NYS MMC Pharmacy Benefit Information Center is a website that assists providers in identifying which
medications are included on the NYS MMC formularies, and can be found at:
https://mmcdruginformation.nysdoh.suny.edu/. This includes a comprehensive listing of “Drugs to Treat
Chemical Dependence” showing formulary coverage by plan. To find this list, select “DRUG QUICKLISTS” from
the page headings, then select the “Therapeutic Classes, Other” tab whereupon a report can be customized to
show coverage by all or only select plans.

policy.authority: New York State Department of Health

provider_manual.text:
Multiple Source Drugs
Reimbursement is only available for those multiple source drugs contained on the List of Medicaid Reimbursable
Drugs.
For certain brand name prescriptions not in the Brand Less Than Generic Program, to be eligible for reimbursement,
prescribers must certify that the brand name drug is required by:
• writing directly on the face of the prescription “Brand Necessary” or “Brand Medically Necessary” in their own
handwriting in addition to the “DAW”, or
• in the case of electronic prescriptions, the prescriber must insert an electronic direction to clarify “Brand
Medically Necessary” or “Brand Necessary”.
provider_manual.title: New York State Medicaid Program: Pharmacy Manual - Policy Guidelines
provider_manual.authority: New York State Department of Health

provider_manual.text:
• The NYRx provides reimbursement for prescription drugs included on the NY Medicaid Pharmacy List of
Reimbursable Drugs, which can be found at: https://www.emedny.org/info/formfile.aspx.
• Prescriptions must be dispensed and billed by a Medicaid enrolled pharmacy, using the member’s individual
Medicaid Client Identification Number (CIN).
Smoking Cessation Policy
• Smoking cessation therapy consists of certain prescription and non-prescription agents.
• Some smoking cessation therapies may be used together. Professional judgment should be exercised when
dispensing multiple smoking cessation products.
• For all smoking cessation products, the member must have a prescription or fiscal order.
• NY Medicaid reimburses for over-the-counter nicotine patches included on the Medicaid Pharmacy List of
Reimbursable Drugs: https://www.emedny.org/info/formfile.aspx.
Emergency Contraception Drug Policy
Both prescription and OTC Emergency Contraception are a covered benefit for all Medicaid members without age
restriction. This includes individuals enrolled in the Family Planning Benefit Program.
provider_manual.title: New York State Medicaid Program: Pharmacy Manual - Policy Guidelines
provider_manual.authority: New York State Department of Health

provider_manual.text:
These orders must be
kept on file at the pharmacy.
• If billing for both the drug and administration, these claims must be submitted separately.
• For the drug, the claim must be submitted using the ordering prescriber’s NPI in the Prescriber ID field. The
pharmacy will be reimbursed for the medication when billed via National Drug Code (NDC) using
reimbursement methodology.
• For the administration, the claim must be submitted using procedure code 96372, and the pharmacy’s NPI
in the Prescriber ID field. A pharmacy will be reimbursed the current administration fee.
provider_manual.title: New York State Medicaid Program: Pharmacy Manual - Policy Guidelines
provider_manual.authority: New York State Department of Health

provider_manual.text:
Note: Reimbursable drugs are listed alphabetically in sections by Rx Type.
Drug Coverage Limitations
Medicaid only provides reimbursement for drugs included on the New York State List of Medicaid Reimbursable Drugs
(unless provided by a facility which includes the cost of drugs in their all-inclusive rate). The following are examples of
drugs/drug uses which are not reimbursable by Medicaid in accordance with Policy and/or state or federal Legislation:
• Drugs used for the treatment of anorexia, weight loss or weight gain pursuant to SSA §1927(d)(2).
• Drugs for the treatment of sexual dysfunction pursuant to SSA §1927(d)(2), and Social Services Law §365-a(4)(f).
• Drugs without a federal rebate agreement pursuant to SSA §1927(a).
• Drugs indicated for cosmetic use or hair growth pursuant to SSA §1927(d)(2).
• Drugs that are not approved as safe and effective by the FDA pursuant to SSA §1927(d)(4)(C).
provider_manual.title: New York State Medicaid Program: Pharmacy Manual - Policy Guidelines
provider_manual.authority: New York State Department of Health

answers.headline_summary: The provider manual, with an effective date of October 1, 2025, is more recent than the newest policy update from February 1, 2024, and provides more detailed information on the general reimbursement process for drugs, including how pharmacies are reimbursed for medications. Policy documents specifically address naloxone coverage, including over-the-counter (OTC) naloxone, and its exemption from prior authorization for emergencies, while the provider manual outlines general drug reimbursement procedures.
answers.policy_definition: NYS Medicaid covers naloxone over the counter (OTC) when prescribed by a practitioner for a NYS Medicaid member or dispensed in compliance with the State-issued naloxone standing order [New York State Medicaid Update February 2024 Volume 40 Number 2:4, 5 — February 1, 2024]. Medicaid policy also requires Medicaid fee-for-service (FFS) and Medicaid Managed Care (MMC) to provide at least five days’ coverage for emergencies, without prior authorization, for medications used to treat substance use disorders, which includes naloxone [New York State Medicaid Update May 2020 Volume 36 Number 10:7, 8 — May 1, 2020].
answers.provider_manual_definition: The NYRx provides reimbursement for prescription drugs included on the NY Medicaid Pharmacy List of Reimbursable Drugs [New York State Medicaid Program: Pharmacy Manual - Policy Guidelines:31 — October 1, 2025]. Pharmacies are reimbursed for the medication when billed via National Drug Code (NDC) using reimbursement methodology [New York State Medicaid Program: Pharmacy Manual - Policy Guidelines:15 — October 1, 2025]. Reimbursement is only available for multiple source drugs on the List of Medicaid Reimbursable Drugs [New York State Medicaid Program: Pharmacy Manual - Policy Guidelines:33 — October 1, 2025]. Medicaid only provides reimbursement for drugs included on the New York State List of Medicaid Reimbursable Drugs, unless provided by a facility that includes the cost of drugs in their all-inclusive rate [New York State Medicaid Program: Pharmacy Manual - Policy Guidelines:28 — October 1, 2025].
similarities: Both sources indicate that reimbursement is provided for covered medications, with the provider manual specifying that drugs must be on the NY Medicaid Pharmacy List of Reimbursable Drugs [New York State Medicaid Program: Pharmacy Manual - Policy Guidelines:31 — October 1, 2025] and policy confirming naloxone is covered [New York State Medicaid Update February 2024 Volume 40 Number 2:4, 5 — February 1, 2024].
differences: Policy documents specifically address naloxone, including its coverage as an over-the-counter (OTC) medication when prescribed or dispensed under a standing order [New York State Medicaid Update February 2024 Volume 40 Number 2:4, 5 — February 1, 2024], and its exemption from prior authorization for emergencies [New York State Medicaid Update May 2020 Volume 36 Number 10:7, 8 — May 1, 2020]. The provider manual, effective October 1, 2025, provides general guidelines for drug reimbursement, focusing on billing via NDC and the requirement for drugs to be on the List of Medicaid Reimbursable Drugs, without specific mention of naloxone or its unique coverage rules [New York State Medicaid Program: Pharmacy Manual - Policy Guidelines:15, 31, 33 — October 1, 2025].

policy.text:
May 2020 New York State Medicaid Update pg. 7

Reminder: Substance Use Disorder Drug Treatment:
Medicaid Managed Care and Fee-for-Service Coverage
Important Legislation Regarding Drugs to Treat Substance Use Disorders:
• Medicaid policy requires Medicaid fee-for-service (FFS) and Medicaid Managed Care (MMC) to provide
at least five days’ coverage for emergencies, without prior authorization, for medications used to
treat substance use disorders. This includes medication associated with the management of opioid
withdrawal and/or stabilization as well as medication used for opioid overdose reversal.
• As published in the August 2016 issue of the Medicaid Update, New York State Social Services Law
§364-j and Public Health Law §273, prohibit prior authorization under Medicaid FFS and MMC for
initial or renewal prescriptions for preferred or formulary forms of buprenorphine or injectable
naltrexone when used for detoxification or maintenance treatment of opioid addiction. The
frequency, quantity, and/or duration limits supported by the Food and Drug Administration (FDA) and
Compendia may continue to be applied.
policy.title: New York State Medicaid Update May 2020 Volume 36 Number 10
policy.authority: New York State Department of Health

policy.text:
December 2022
Volume 38 | Number 14
Opioid Overdose Prevention
The New York State (NYS) Department of Health (DOH) has recently released two brochures on Naloxone,
the life-saving medication that can reverse opioid overdose, for individuals who are pregnant or who’ve
recently given birth, as well as their support person(s):
• Patient education brochure tilted Do You or Someone You Know Take Opioids?
policy.title: New York State Medicaid Update December 2022 Volume 38 Number 14
policy.authority: New York State Department of Health

policy.text:
February 2024 New York State Medicaid Update pg. 4

Reminder: Pharmacy Coverage Includes Over the Counter Naloxone
New York State (NYS) Medicaid covers naloxone over the counter (OTC) when prescribed by the practitioner
of a NYS Medicaid member or dispensed to a NYS Medicaid member in compliance with the State-issued
naloxone standing order. Information regarding the naloxone standing order can be found in the NYS
Department of Health (DOH) Non-Patient Specific Prescription for Naloxone with Pharmacy Dispensing
Protocol document, located at: https://www.health.ny.gov/diseases/aids/general/opioid_overdose_prevention
/docs/naloxone_standing_order_pharmacies.pdf.
To comply with NYS laws, regulations, and NYS Medicaid policy, a pharmacy must submit a claim and
dispense naloxone OTC pursuant to a fiscal order or via a standing order.
policy.title: New York State Medicaid Update February 2024 Volume 40 Number 2
policy.authority: New York State Department of Health

policy.text:
Reminder: Substance Use Disorder Drug Treatment,
Medicaid Managed Care and Fee-for-Service Coverage
Social Services Law §364-j and Public Health Law §273 prohibits prior authorization under Medicaid fee-for-
service (FFS) and Medicaid Managed Care (MMC) for initial or renewal prescriptions for preferred or formulary
forms of buprenorphine or injectable naltrexone when used for detoxification or maintenance treatment of opioid
addiction. The Food and Drug Administration (FDA) and Compendia-supported frequency quantity and/or
duration limits may continue to be applied.
The New York State (NYS) MMC Pharmacy Benefit Information Center
The NYS MMC Pharmacy Benefit Information Center is a website that assists providers in identifying which
medications are included on the NYS MMC formularies, and can be found at:
https://mmcdruginformation.nysdoh.suny.edu/. This includes a comprehensive listing of “Drugs to Treat
Chemical Dependence” showing formulary coverage by plan. To find this list, select “DRUG QUICKLISTS” from
the page headings, then select the “Therapeutic Classes, Other” tab whereupon a report can be customized to
show coverage by all or only select plans.

answers.headline_summary: The provider manual, with an effective date of October 1, 2025, is more recent than the newest policy document, which is dated February 1, 2024. Therefore, the provider manual should receive more weight regarding how naloxone is reimbursed. Both sources indicate that naloxone is covered by Medicaid, with the provider manual detailing general reimbursement for prescription drugs and the policy specifically addressing over-the-counter naloxone coverage.
answers.policy_definition: New York State (NYS) Medicaid covers naloxone over the counter (OTC) when prescribed by a practitioner for a NYS Medicaid member or dispensed to a NYS Medicaid member in compliance with the State-issued naloxone standing order [New York State Medicaid Update February 2024 Volume 40 Number 2:4, 5 — February 1, 2024]. Medicaid policy also requires Medicaid fee-for-service (FFS) and Medicaid Managed Care (MMC) to provide at least five days’ coverage for emergencies, without prior authorization, for medications used to treat substance use disorders, which includes medication associated with management [New York State Medicaid Update May 2020 Volume 36 Number 10:7, 8 — May 1, 2020].
answers.provider_manual_definition: Reimbursement for prescription drugs, including naloxone, is available for those included on the NY Medicaid Pharmacy List of Reimbursable Drugs [New York State Medicaid Program: Pharmacy Manual - Policy Guidelines:31 — October 1, 2025]. Prescriptions must be dispensed and billed by a Medicaid enrolled pharmacy, using the member’s individual Medicaid Client Identification Number (CIN) [New York State Medicaid Program: Pharmacy Manual - Policy Guidelines:31 — October 1, 2025]. When billing for the drug, the claim must be submitted using the ordering prescriber’s NPI in the Prescriber ID field, and the pharmacy will be reimbursed for the medication when billed via National Drug Code (NDC) using reimbursement methodology [New York State Medicaid Program: Pharmacy Manual - Policy Guidelines:15 — October 1, 2025]. If billing for both the drug and administration, these claims must be submitted separately [New York State Medicaid Program: Pharmacy Manual - Policy Guidelines:15 — October 1, 2025].
similarities: Both sources indicate that naloxone is covered by New York State Medicaid [New York State Medicaid Update February 2024 Volume 40 Number 2:4, 5 — February 1, 2024] and [New York State Medicaid Program: Pharmacy Manual - Policy Guidelines:31 — October 1, 2025].
differences: The policy documents specifically mention coverage for over-the-counter (OTC) naloxone when prescribed or dispensed under a standing order [New York State Medicaid Update February 2024 Volume 40 Number 2:4, 5 — February 1, 2024], while the provider manual focuses on general reimbursement procedures for prescription drugs on the Medicaid Reimbursable Drugs list [New York State Medicaid Program: Pharmacy Manual - Policy Guidelines:31 — October 1, 2025].

The provider manual details the specific billing requirements for reimbursement, including the use of the member's CIN, prescriber's NPI, and NDC for the drug, and separate billing for administration [New York State Medicaid Program: Pharmacy Manual - Policy Guidelines:15, 31 — October 1, 2025]. The policy documents do not provide these granular billing instructions.

Policy documents mention that Medicaid policy requires at least five days’ coverage for emergencies for medications used to treat substance use disorders, without prior authorization [New York State Medicaid Update May 2020 Volume 36 Number 10:7, 8 — May 1, 2020], a detail not present in the provided provider manual excerpts.
